danger_signals.visual_form: Screen transition to combat arena, enemy sprites appear
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat; player must engage in combat or flee

danger_signals.visual_form: Dark, enclosed spaces with maze-like layouts within temple structures
danger_signals.meaning: Increased danger; navigation hazard and encounter density

danger_signals.visual_form: Large sprite variations for story-specific characters (Miriene, Reeshine, Lux, Kamiwoo, Meisia, Tokio)
danger_signals.meaning: Major story threat; escalated combat challenge tied to world progression

safety_signals.visual_form: Populated areas with NPCs, shops, and structured layouts within worlds
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone for resource gathering, healing, and information gathering

safety_signals.visual_form: Central temple structure as interconnected world portal
safety_signals.meaning: Safe sanctuary; player respawn/reset point and narrative anchor

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible partner representations when Power Ark and Wisdom Ark are obtained
safety_signals.meaning: Gameplay safety/progression; mechanical advantage through ally selection

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Witch character sprite with distinct appearance tied to her story world
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: World-specific environment associated with magic/sorcery themes

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Grappler character sprite; martial combat specialist visual design
Master warrior character sprite; skilled fighter appearance
Large monster/ogre sprite; non-humanoid intimidating appearance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Closed or inaccessible dungeon entrance within story world; sealed by narrative gate
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs may reference locked areas; blocked pathways prevent progression

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Active story world (player exploring specific character's realm)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Thematic environmental design; character-specific color palette and architecture within dungeon/town
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Random encounters spawn; specific boss character awaits in dungeon climax

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual collection indicator; ark object acquired in inventory or interface
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gains access to next story world or progression gate; mechanical unlock

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Temple transitions to reveal final area/boss chamber; visual unlock of previously sealed space
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final dungeon becomes accessible; endgame narrative becomes available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Story world returns to neutral state; character no longer occupies boss chamber
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Area becomes traversable without combat; narrative conclusion for that world

environmental_storytelling.example: Each of the six story characters (Miriene, Reeshine, Lux, Kamiwoo, Meisia, Tokio) has a thematically designed world environment that reflects their archetype—witch worlds show magical aesthetics, grappler worlds show training grounds, ninja worlds show shadows/darkness
Central temple structure serves as anthology anchor; visual anchor between disconnected story worlds communicates that all narratives are contained within one mystical structure
Increasingly complex dungeon layouts within each world communicate escalating narrative stakes; ark placement in dungeon depths signals climactic confrontation ahead
Towns populated with NPCs visually signal safe zones contrasted against empty dungeon corridors that signal danger/isolation

environmental_storytelling.technique: Figurine companions as visual party customization
environmental_storytelling.example: Collectible figurine selections from temple allow player to visually customize party, communicating mechanical autonomy and player choice

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Free movement; can interact with NPCs; no time pressure
Random encounters trigger; movement subject to combat interruption; heightened encounter rate near boss chambers
Stationary in boss chamber; awaits player arrival; no movement or patrol behavior documented
Aggressive combat behavior; uses character-specific abilities tied to archetype (witch spells, grappler throws, ninja techniques)
Stationary dialogue triggers; provide lore exposition, world-state information, and mechanical guidance; no hostility
Spawn on screen transition; initiate combat automatically; grant experience and progression rewards on defeat

notes: Mystic Ark uses a temple-anthology structure where six distinct character-worlds communicate identity primarily through environmental theming and character sprite design rather than explicit color or symbolic systems. World-state communication relies on narrative gates (arks as progression keys) and environmental accessibility (locked vs. unlocked areas). Danger is communicated through environment isolation (dungeons) and encounter systems rather than explicit visual threat indicators. The game lacks documented faction conflict mechanics—characters are story-context antagonists rather than ideological factions. Visual communication is implicit and context-dependent rather than explicit UI-based signaling.
